related_requirements.requirement: List Playlogs (H4), List Connections (H5), List Platform Actions (H6)
related_requirements.explanation: This layer serves as the visual interface where users interact with the system to view listed data such as playlogs, connections, and audit trails.
As the direct point of user interaction, this layer relies on the backend to respond within 5 seconds to prevent user frustration.
Users must be able to access the platform interfaces at any time of day, ensuring continuous availability except during scheduled updates.
This service handles user authentication and authorization, ensuring only users with proper permissions can access specific functionalities.

related_requirements.requirement: List Playlogs (H4), List Connections (H5), List Platform Actions (H6)
related_requirements.explanation: It defines API REST routes and processes incoming requests from the presentation layer, forwarding them to the Analytics Module for data retrieval.
By managing routing and SSL efficiently, it contributes to reducing the overall response time for user actions.
This service executes the main business logic and generates audit trail data for actions performed on the platform (create, edit, delete, publish), sending them to Kinesis.
It validates user permissions via the database before allowing actions that modify platform entities.

related_requirements.requirement: List Playlogs (H4), List Connections (H5), List Platform Actions (H6)
related_requirements.explanation: This module is responsible for obtaining and processing data from AWS services (Athena, S3) and the Database to fulfill listing requests.
It implements logic to mask sensitive information (like admin usernames) in the response data to protect confidential user information.
It optimizes data retrieval by utilizing partitioned queries in Amazon Athena, ensuring responses are returned quickly.
The module is designed to work with serverless AWS services that scale automatically with data volume increases.
This service facilitates bidirectional communication with players, receiving real-time information about content being played.
It processes connection status data received from players to track connectivity.
This service communicates directly with players to receive information regarding player status, content reproduction, and schedules.

related_requirements.requirement: Store Platform Actions/Playlogs/Connections Context (H1, H2, H3)
related_requirements.explanation: Stores structured metadata about users, clients, and players which is necessary for contextualizing the raw data logs.
Stores user permission data which is queried to validate access rights for specific functionalities.
Securely stores confidential user data that requires protection according to privacy requirements.

related_requirements.requirement: Store Playlogs (H1), Store Connections (H2), Store Platform Actions (H3)
related_requirements.explanation: Acts as the central Data Lake repository storing historical documents (JSON) containing playlogs, connections, and audit trails.
Provides object storage capable of supporting an unlimited number of connections and data volume simultaneously.
Automatically scales storage capacity as the volume of data produced by users increases.
Used specifically for storing playlogs in real-time, allowing for immediate access via Composite Keys.
As a NoSQL database, it enables fast read/write operations for active data, contributing to low latency.

related_requirements.requirement: List Playlogs (H4), List Connections (H5), List Platform Actions (H6)
related_requirements.explanation: Allows running SQL queries directly on data stored in S3 to retrieve historical records for listing functionalities.
Being a serverless service, it does not require infrastructure administration when data volume or users increase.
Performs queries in parallel to obtain data quickly, meeting the response time criteria.

related_requirements.requirement: List Playlogs (H4), List Connections (H5), List Platform Actions (H6)
related_requirements.explanation: Maintains the Data Catalog metadata, defining table structures and partitions so Athena can locate and read data correctly.
Manages dynamic partitions to optimize query performance as the dataset grows over time.
Ingests high-volume data streams from microservices (Core, WebSocket) for near real-time transmission.
Handles the throughput of incoming data events from multiple generators without bottlenecking.
Loads the ingested stream data into Amazon S3 buckets automatically based on size or time intervals.
Automatically adjusts data loading mechanisms to accommodate increasing data volumes.
